weather_source: TMYx_Boulder (substituted — weather station not yet installed Aug 2025)
result: UNUSABLE — structural ventilation model failure in pure cooling season
note: GreenLight ventHeat control drives fans to 100% whenever tAir > heatSetPoint + heatDeadZone (75.5°F). Colorado August afternoons routinely exceed this, causing continuous full ventilation, overcooling, and a -4.78°F cold bias. Increasing heatDeadZone to 15°C (heatMax=93.5°F) produced identical results — the ODE converges to the same steady state because any reduction in fan speed causes temp to immediately spike above heatMax. The Vanthoor/Katzin ventilation model assumes venting achieves setpoint; it does not model the outdoor-hotter-than-indoor regime. Additionally, weather was substituted from TMYx (weather station not installed until ~Aug 2025), introducing unknown forcing error. Summer calibration requires: (1) real weather station data and (2) a modified ventilation model with outdoor temperature guard preventing venting when tOut > tAir.
action: exclude from calibration suite; document as known summer limitation
